title: Bridge Essay: Unilateral Stimulation of the Dorsolateral Striatum Attenuates Goal-Directed Act

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.

**Unilateral Stimulation of the Dorsolateral Striatum Attenuates Goal-Directed Action.**. Eur J Neurosci.. PMID: 40771101. DOI: 10.1111/ejn.70175. It has long been known that the dorsomedial (DMS) and dorsolateral striatum (DLS) mediate distinct forms of action control, with DMS mediating goal-directed actions and DLS mediating habits. Recent evidence suggests that, in accord with its role in goal-directed control, unilateral stimulation of dorsomedial striatum (DMS) enhances actions contralateral to the stimulation in amanner that scales with the prior reward history of that action.
